headline: What Is Pig Butchering?
subhead: The dominant scam technique hiding inside 'Investment Related' and 'Imposter Scams'

definition.short: A long-con scam where fraudsters build romantic or investment relationships over weeks, then convince victims to invest in fake crypto platforms.
definition.etymology: Named for the practice of 'fattening the pig before slaughter' - scammers cultivate trust and extract small wins before the final theft.
definition.origin: Originated in China around 2016, scaled globally during COVID via Southeast Asian fraud compounds.

step_1.name: Initial Contact
step_1.description: Wrong-number text, dating app match, or LinkedIn connection. Scammer uses fake identity, often attractive photos.

step_2.name: Relationship Building
step_2.description: Weeks of daily messaging. Scammer shares personal details, builds emotional connection or professional rapport.

step_3.name: Investment Introduction
step_3.description: Scammer casually mentions crypto trading success. Shows fake platform with impressive returns.

step_4.name: Small Wins
step_4.description: Victim invests small amount, sees gains, successfully withdraws. Builds trust and confidence.

step_5.name: The Butchering
step_5.description: Victim invests large sum. Platform freezes account, demands 'taxes' or 'fees.' Scammer disappears.

why_it_works.psychological: Exploits loneliness, greed, and trust. Victims often too embarrassed to report.
why_it_works.technical: Fake platforms look legitimate. Small initial withdrawals build false confidence.
why_it_works.scale: Fraud compounds in Southeast Asia use trafficked workers to run thousands of simultaneous scams.

sources.name: Cyvers Web3 Security Report
sources.finding: $5.5B in Ethereum losses across 200,000 cases in 2024
